body:
{
  "title": "Tatum Nguyen — Data Engineer, Anchor Codec",
  "compiled_truth": "Tatum Nguyen is a data engineer at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), where she works on building and maintaining the internal data pipelines that support the company's deterministic data infrastructure. She joined in March 2027 after spending roughly five years bouncing between analytics roles at mid-stage startups, where she earned a reputation as someone who could migrate legacy data systems to structured-data pipelines without disrupting production workloads. Before Anchor, she had done exactly this at three different companies — each time arriving to find a mess of ad-hoc scripts and leaving behind clean, well-documented pipeline architectures.

Tatum is known internally for being analytical to a fault — she'll run numbers on almost anything before committing to a decision — but paradoxically she ships extremely fast. Colleagues joke that she'll spend 20 minutes modeling the optimal approach and then have a working prototype pushed before lunch. This combination made her a natural fit for the engineering culture at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), which prizes both rigor and velocity in its infrastructure work.

Her day-to-day involves wrangling the telemetry and performance data generated by Anchor's single-pass primitives, structuring it so that other teams can query against it reliably. She also maintains several of the internal dashboards used by the go-to-market team to track pipeline throughput and customer-facing metrics. One of her quieter contributions has been standardizing how Anchor stores and versions internal datasets — a project that didn't get much fanfare but saved the team significant debugging time in Q3 2027.

Before joining Anchor, Tatum studied applied math at a state university and stumbled into data work almost by accident, taking a part-time analytics gig during her senior year that turned into a full-time role. She's mentioned in a few internal retrospectives that she was drawn to Anchor specifically because the deterministic nature of the stack appealed to her — fewer edge cases, fewer surprises, more time spent on actual engineering rather then firefighting.

Outside of work, Tatum is a competitive rock climber and occasionally posts beta videos from local gyms. She's based in the Pacific Northwest and works remotely, syncing with the core team via async standups.",
  "timeline": "- **2027-03-01** | Tatum Nguyen joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as a Data Engineer, onboarding remotely from the Pacific Northwest.
- **2027-03-18** | Completes initial audit of Anchor's internal data pipelines; flags several inconsistencies in how telemetry data is partitioned.
- **2027-05-02** | Ships first version of the internal performance dashboard used by the go-to-market team to track pipeline throughput.
- **2027-06-14** | Begins dataset versioning project, proposing a standardized schema for all internal data stores.
- **2027-08-22** | Dataset versioning rollout complete; credited in internal retro with reducing debugging time by roughly 30%.
- **2027-10-09** | Presents at an internal engineering all-hands on best practices for structured telemetry ingestion.
- **2027-12-01** | Takes on mentorship of a new junior data engineer joining the team.
- **2028-02-15** | Leads migration of legacy analytics queries to a new structured pipeline format, completing the work two weeks ahead of schedule.
- **2028-04-07** | Participates in cross-team planning session for upcoming customer-facing analytics features at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor)."
}
